mission.title: Install and Configure Kyverno (Policy Engine)
mission.description: Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that enables security, compliance, automation, and governance through policy-as-code. It is ideal for teams looking to enforce policies on Kubernetes resources without needing to learn a new language. Key trade-offs include the need for careful policy management to avoid unintended consequences.

steps.title: Add the Kyverno Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kyverno Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Kyverno.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kyverno with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Kyverno in the 'kyverno' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kyverno

helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

```

> Creates a namespace for Kyverno.
> Installs Kyverno in the created namespace.

steps.title: Verify Kyverno installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kyverno pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Lists all pods in the kyverno namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a sample policy
steps.description:
This command creates a sample policy to demonstrate Kyverno's functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

```

> Creates a policy that enforces the presence of the 'app' label on Pods.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

steps.title: Verify the policy
steps.description:
This command checks if the policy is applied correctly by creating a Pod without the required label.

```bash

kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --restart=Never

kubectl get events

```

> Attempts to create a Pod without the required label to test the policy.
> Check events to see if the policy enforcement was triggered.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kyverno
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kyverno and all associated resources. Be aware that any policies created will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno

kubectl delete namespace kyverno

```

> Uninstalls the Kyverno release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kyverno
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your policies. This step upgrades Kyverno to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusterpolicies -o yaml > clusterpolicies-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Backs up existing policies to a file.
> Upgrades Kyverno to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a policy is enforcing rules that are not being met by the resource.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the resource being created meets the policy requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Kyverno pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the Kyverno deployment.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kyverno pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kyverno' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Policy not being enforced
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The policy may not be applied correctly or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the policy configuration and ensure it is applied using 'kubectl get clusterpolicies'.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to create resources due to policy enforcement
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** A policy is blocking resource creation due to unmet conditions.

**Fix:**
Review the policy rules and ensure that the resource meets the specified criteria.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new policy types and improved validation mechanisms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kyverno pods running in the 'kyverno' namespace and the sample policy enforced correctly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
